回上一頁	國立金門大學	 
 	教學綱要	 

部別 : 日間部學士班	113學年度第1學期	列印日期 : 2026/05/24
科目名稱 : 教育心理學 (Educational Psychology)	開課班級 : 應英四乙	學　　分 : 2.0	授課時數 : 2.0
授課教師 : 李盈興	必選修 : 選修	 	 


1.教學目標
This course covers the diverse yet significant issues in Educational Psychology. The first half of the course aims to familiarize students with three theoretic perspectives of Educational Psychology: behaviorism, nativism, and constructionism, and the supplementary teaching strategies that can motivate students in their acquisition processes underlying the different perspectives. The second half of the course brings students to the extended yet deepened discussion on Educational Psychology (not limited to the context of learning classrooms). The issues include cognitive (self, social, and moral) development, learner differences and needs, first and second language acquisition, cultural diversity, and teaching pedagogies and assessments. Students are encouraged to consider their previous learning experiences in retrospect to sort out the theoretic differentiation. Furthermore, they must actively construct their own course syllabus (not limited to the English course) by contrasting or synthesizing different approaches and methods in the classroom in order to arrive at the more comprehensive understanding of Educational Psychology, or acquisition and teaching in general. 


2.教學綱要
The course of the semester is outlined –
1. Behavioral Views of Learning
2. Cognitive Views of Learning
3. Complex Cognitive Processes
4. Constructivism and Designing Learning Environments
5. Social Cognitive Views of Learning and Motivation
6. Motivation in Learning and Teaching
7. Cognitive Development
8. The Self, Social, and Moral Development
9. Learner Differences and Learning Needs
10. Language Development, Language Diversity, and Immigrant Education
11. Culture and Diversity
12. Managing Learning Environments
13. Teaching Every Student
14. Classroom Assessment, Grading, and Standardized Testing

6.成績評定及課堂要求
Evaluations: Exams 60%; Presentations 30%; Participation 10%. 
Requirements: Students should participate in two exams and two presentations. Students are responsible for their grades. No tardiness or no absence without reason will be accepted.
